finding: Scheduling uses Pacific Time for an Australian business, forcing the user to do timezone math to know when the page will publish

evidence.quote: Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles). Leave blank unless this page should publish later.

persona_rationale: I already translate language in my head; making me also convert Pacific Time to my own timezone to know when my page goes live is a real risk of publishing at the wrong hour.

suggested_fix.current: Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles). Leave blank unless this page should publish later.
suggested_fix.proposed: Uses your local time (Australia/Adelaide). The page will go live at this time.

finding: After saving a schedule the date/time field clears and no 'Scheduled for {date}' confirmation appears anywhere

evidence.quote: field shows empty after save, no confirmation

persona_rationale: Without a confirmation sentence I cannot tell success from failure, so I assume it failed and try again, risking a duplicate schedule.

suggested_fix.proposed: Show explicit text after save: 'Scheduled to publish on Wed 11 Jun 2026, 12:00 (Australia/Adelaide)'

finding: Two different messages describe the same publish gate at the same moment

evidence.quote: Chip said "Fix SEO title" while readiness panel said "Add a hero headline before publishing."

persona_rationale: Conflicting English sentences make me distrust my own reading and fix the wrong thing first.

suggested_fix.current: Chip 'Fix SEO title' vs panel 'Add a hero headline before publishing.'
suggested_fix.proposed: Show one consistent message listing all gates in both places: 'Before publishing: add a hero headline and an SEO title.'

finding: Blocker text 'Fix content 1 · Cta Label' uses an internal block index and an unexpanded abbreviation
persona_rationale: 'content 1' is not a name I gave anything and 'Cta' reads as a typo rather than 'CTA', so I cannot map the message to a field.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a button label to the Hero section

finding: Blocker names a 'Destination URL' field that is not on the canvas — it is hidden in a block settings modal, and the message gives no location

evidence.quote: the destination field is NOT on the canvas; it lives in Block actions → Edit settings modal

persona_rationale: The message gives no location hint; without it a non-native reader cannot find the field at all.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a destination URL — open Block actions → Edit settings on the Hero block

finding: 'Eyebrow' is used as a content-field label with no explanation
persona_rationale: 'Eyebrow' is a culturally-specific design metaphor; translated literally it is a body part and means nothing as a field name.

finding: 'CTA' appears throughout the tool but is never expanded on first use

evidence.quote: Article-style page with a strong introduction and CTA.

persona_rationale: An unexpanded abbreviation forces me to guess; expanding it once removes the guesswork.

suggested_fix.proposed: Call to action (CTA) button on first use, then 'CTA' thereafter

finding: Readiness is communicated only by colored dots in the table cell, with no visible text label

evidence.quote: Readiness column shows only colored dots, no text
cell "SEO readiness: Needs work": button "SEO readiness: Needs work"

persona_rationale: Color carries different cultural meaning and I cannot read a dot; I need the word printed on screen, not only in an aria label or tooltip.

suggested_fix.current: Colored dot only (text in aria label)
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a visible text label beside the dot: yellow → 'Needs work', red → 'Blocked', green → 'Ready'

finding: Workflow filter and readiness labels are ambiguous internal states ('Updating', 'Needs links', 'Opportunities')

evidence.quote: All metadata Needs review Updating Needs links Metadata issues Scheduled Schedule failed

persona_rationale: 'Updating' could mean the system is busy, and 'Opportunities' as a status is a business idiom I cannot map to a page's health.

suggested_fix.proposed: Being edited / Missing internal links / Could be improved

finding: Invalid-path error message is engineer jargon, not actionable for a normal user
persona_rationale: 'root-relative' is a developer term; the message should restate the placeholder example so anyone can fix it without that knowledge.

suggested_fix.proposed: The path must start with a slash, like /resources/old-page

finding: On invalid submit the entered Old path / Destination values are cleared instead of preserved
persona_rationale: Re-typing after an error is doubly costly for me because I read and type English slowly; losing my input is punishing.

suggested_fix.current: Form clears the typed values on validation error
suggested_fix.proposed: Keep the typed Old path and Destination values after a validation error

finding: Same publish event shows two different times (AM vs PM) across editor and revision preview
persona_rationale: Two different times for one event make me think I'm looking at different versions, so I cannot trust which revision is which.

suggested_fix.current: Editor 'Jun 10, 6:19 AM' vs revision page 'Jun 10, 3:49 PM'
suggested_fix.proposed: Use one timestamp format and timezone everywhere, e.g. 'Published 10 Jun 2026, 16:19 (Australia/Adelaide)'

finding: Revision label is a bare lowercase verb that reads like an unfinished code string
persona_rationale: A lowercase bare 'publish' looks like a code key leaked into the UI; I cannot tell if it is a label or a bug.

finding: Revisions empty state uses stacked noun-phrase triggers that are hard to parse for a non-native reader

evidence.quote: Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.

persona_rationale: 'library refreshes' and 'draft restores' as nouns are ambiguous; verb-form sentences are clearer for me.

suggested_fix.current: Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.
suggested_fix.proposed: A revision is saved each time you publish the page, refresh its content libraries, or restore a draft.

finding: 'Governance comments' and 'Builder support' are abstract corporate phrases
persona_rationale: 'Governance' is an abstract business word; 'Internal notes' tells me plainly what the box is for.
